title: Friesodielsia fornicata
intro: Friesodielsia fornicata, also known as Friesodielsia, is a medicinal plant species belonging to the family Annonaceae, commonly found in tropical regions of Asia. The plant is valued for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antidiabetic properties, which may contribute to the management of various diseases. Bioactive constituents isolated from Friesodielsia fornicata include alkaloids, flavonoids, and phenolic compounds, such as friedelanone and dielsin, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. Traditionally, Friesodielsia fornicata is used to make herbal preparations, including decoctions and infusions, which are used to treat various ailments, including fever, rheumatism, and respiratory issues.
taxonomy: Friesodielsia fornicata is classified within the family Annonaceae, which is a family of flowering plants commonly known as the custard apple family. This classification places Friesodielsia fornicata in the order Magnoliales and the clade magnoliids. At the kingdom level, it is classified as Plantae, while at the phylum level it is classified as Magnoliophyta. The class is Magnoliopsida, and the order is Magnoliales. Further classification places Friesodielsia fornicata in the family Annonaceae, subfamily Annonoideae, tribe Annoneae.
